response.justification: Strong technical foundation with 19 years of diverse development experience, but fundamentally misaligned with role requirements. Resume shows individual contributor progression to Staff level with no evidence of people management or technical team leadership—both critical minimums for this Level II Manager position. While infrastructure/DevOps exposure (GitLab, CI/CD, Docker) provides some developer tools context, lacks the focused 3-year build-systems and test-automation emphasis. The role demands full-time people-leadership (one-on-ones, performance management, team development); this candidate shows knowledge-sharing initiative but zero management track record.
strengths: Exceptional technical breadth across 19 years: languages, platforms, and infrastructure (CI/CD, GitLab, Docker, cloud), enabling rapid learning in developer tools domain
Demonstrated passion for engineering culture and knowledge sharing: workshops, Refactoring Dojos, open-source contributions, and teaching—signals alignment with mentoring values
Infrastructure and DevOps exposure through Altom role (GitLab administration, CI servers, artifact management) shows practical understanding of developer productivity tooling
concerns: No people management experience: zero evidence of hiring, 1-on-1s, performance feedback, or team development—a minimum requirement that cannot be recovered in the interview
Career trajectory is individual contributor (IC) to Staff engineer, not technical leader to manager; no signs of directing teams, architectural ownership, or organizational change leadership
Developer tools expertise is shallow and scattered: brief infrastructure work at Altom, but no sustained 3-year focus on compilers, automated releases, or test automation systems as required

key_factors.factor: Developer tools and build systems depth
